items.title: RTD detains three Rohingya men for traffic offences in Kedah
items.summary: Two Rohingya men were detained under Op PeWA in Kota Setar, while another was held in Bandar Baharu for various traffic offences, including driving without valid licences. – Source: RTD ALOR STAR: Three Rohingya men were detained by the Road Transport Department (RTD) in separate operations across Kedah yesterday for various traffic offences, including driving without valid licences. Two of the men, both in their 40s, were detained under Op PeWA, which targets foreign nationals driving without valid licences, insurance or proper documentation.

items.title: Storm tears through Ipoh, damages over 200 homes in Anjung Bercham (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, June 19 — A violent afternoon storm ripped through Anjung Bercham here, damaging more than 200 homes across seven residential areas and triggering emergency response teams on the ground. “Acting on the information, three APM personnel were deployed to monitor the affected areas,” it said. Among the affected locations were: Dataran Tasek Timur 6 (177 houses) Hala Tasek Timur 1 (22 houses) Lorong Bercham 19 (22 houses) Hala Tasek Timur 34 (10 houses) Tasek Timur 15 (five houses) Lintasan Tasek Timur 1 (two houses) Lintasan Tasek Timur 3 (two houses) The Ipoh City Council (MBI) deployed emergency teams at 5.05pm to clear fallen trees and debris blocking roads and posing risks to residents.

items.title: 111 investors sue QEW Group, directors for RM20.5 million over failed scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As many as 111 investors have filed a lawsuit at the High Court against an investment company and its two directors for failing to return their investment capital totalling RM20.5 million. The largest single investment among them amounted to RM1.7 million. All the investors, acting as plaintiffs, claimed that QEW Group Bhd failed to fulfill its responsibilities in managing the investment scheme and began delaying, reducing, and eventually halting dividend payments entirely without any reasonable financial explanation.

items.title: Deadly flare-up in Lebanon rattles US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: GENEVA: Deadly exchanges between Israel and Hizbollah in Lebanon put a newly signed deal to end the Middle East war under further strain on Friday, after the postponement of planned US-Iran talks in Switzerland. Lebanon said 18 people were killed Friday in Israeli airstrikes in the south, while Israel's military reported four troops were killed, drawing furious reactions at home. France, whose President Emmanuel Macron presided over Trump's signing of the deal in Versailles, urged Israel to "respect" the accord, Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said.

items.title: E&O, Cengild and Skyspring team up for RM350mil Penang private hospital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eastern & Oriental Bhd (E&O) has partnered with Cengild Medical Bhd and Skyspring Sdn Bhd to develop a multi-disciplinary tertiary private hospital at the Gurney Green district within Andaman Island, Penang. The company said its subsidiary KCB Holdings Sdn Bhd had entered into a conditional joint venture agreement with Cengild Medical and Skyspring for the project. KCB will hold a 30 per cent stake in the joint venture company, while Skyspring and Cengild Medical will own 45 per cent and 25 per cent respectively.

items.title: Annuar: Pas never pushed Bersatu out of Perikatan Nasional
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas has never sought to force Bersatu out of Perikatan Nasional (PN), despite the apparent strain in relations between the two parties. Pas central committee member Tan Sri Annuar Musa said Pas had never raised the issue of expelling Bersatu from the coalition and was instead focused on addressing the challenges currently facing PN. The PN information chief said all component parties should prioritise unity and work towards resolving differences for the benefit of the coalition.

items.title: Hormuz ship traffic climbs after war deal: trackers
items.summary: PARIS: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose to its highest rate in two months after a deal to halt the US-Iran war, maritime trackers said today. A total of 25 commercial vessels crossed the newly reopened strait on Thursday, the highest number since mid-April, according to data from tracking firm AXSMarine. Before the war, about 120 vessels a day passed through the strait, according to shipping journal Lloyd's List.

items.title: MyCC, DOSM ink agreement to strengthen data sharing, economic analysis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) and the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) have signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) to strengthen strategic cooperation in data sharing, economic analysis and capacity building. The signing was witnessed by MyCC chief executive officer Datuk Iskandar Ismail and DOSM Deputy Chief Statistician (Economic Programmes) Siti Asiah Ahmad. MyCC said in a statement that the collaboration signifies the government's commitment to adopt a data-centric approach to strengthen the implementation of government policies, including enforcement efforts related to competition issues in the country.

items.title: Nhaveen murder case: Federal Court strikes out prosecution appeal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court today struck out the prosecution's notice of appeal against the acquittal of a man charged with murdering bullying victim T. Nhaveen nine years ago. I seek further directions from the court," said Allan. The appellate court remitted the case to the Penang High Court for the four men to enter their defence.

items.title: Queen: Mutual respect among foundations of harmony upheld by Malaysians
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mutual respect is among the foundations of harmony that Malaysians have always upheld, Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, said. "Her Majesty reminded particularly the Muslims in the country of a hadith narrated by Al-Bukhari," the post said. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honour his guest.

items.title: Investors may face a trickier second half after resilient first half: Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered global chief investment officer Steve Brice, however, said investors are likely to face a more complex environment in the second half of the year. Standard Chartered's Wealth Solutions chief investment office (CIO) has released its Global Market Outlook for the second half of 2026, outlining its investment strategy and key themes for investors navigating an evolving market landscape. The CIO expects risk assets to remain supported by a soft-landing macroeconomic environment despite heightened volatility stemming from energy prices, equity supply, investor positioning and central bank policy.

items.title: Bukit Bintang to get new mosque by early next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A new mosque capable of accommodating more than 1,500 worshippers will be built in Bukit Bintang and is expected to be completed early next year, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. He said the mosque will replace the Outreach Oasis Bukit Bintang (OOBB) community centre, currently used as a temporary Friday prayer surau, following approval by the Federal Territories Syariah Law Consultative Committee on June 8. "The project is being carried out with the cooperation of various parties, including the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (JAWI), the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), and several non‑governmental organisations," he added.– BERNAMA © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Gaza ceasefire a 'deadly illusion': UNICEF
items.summary: GENEVA: The ceasefire declared in Gaza more than eight months ago is a "deadly illusion", the UN charged today, with 265 children killed there since the fighting was supposed to have stopped. The UN children's agency said the number of Palestinian children killed since the ceasefire was announced between Israel and Hamas, which runs Gaza, was an "absurd and devastating figure." Elder stressed that the children killed since the ceasefire was declared "were not killed in a war zone."

items.title: Three Zahid media officers escape serious injury in Maran accident
items.summary: MARAN: Three media officers attached to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi were among seven people injured in a crash involving three vehicles at Km144 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) early today. The 1am accident involved a Toyota Fortuner carrying four occupants — a driver and three media officers — and two lorries. Maran police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said all three vehicles were travelling towards Kuala Lumpur when the crash occurred.

items.title: Two men freed from drug trafficking charges after 5 years behind bars
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A former lorry driver was acquitted by the High Court today after spending five years in detention on drug trafficking charges. Shafie Yakob, 40, said he delivered a package for an acquaintance and had no reason to suspect it contained illegal substances. He was released after High Court judge Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar, at the end of the prosecution case, acquitted him and co-accused welder Mohd Sofian Tomingan, 48, of trafficking 141,147.6g of cannabis.

items.title: Melaka eyes new flights to Batam, Kota Baru and Penang
items.summary: MELAKA: Three new routes, one international and two domestic, are expected to open via Melaka International Airport (LTAM), Batu Berendam, by year‑end. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said the proposed routes are Melaka–Batam (Indonesia), Melaka–Kota Baru and Melaka–Penang. We intend to realise them this year, subject to airline assessments," he told reporters after visiting LTAM in conjunction with the Melaka–Pekanbaru–Melaka flight today.

items.title: Trading of Ajinomoto's shares to be suspended on Monday pending announcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Trading of Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd's (AMB) shares will be suspended from 9am to 5pm on Monday pending an announcement. "Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd has approved AMB's request to suspend the trading of its ordinary shares with effect from 9am to 5pm on Monday, June 22, 2026, pending the release of a material announcement. "The request for suspension is made under subparagraph 3.1(b)(i) of Practice Note 2 on Requests for Suspension of the Main Market Listing Requirements of Bursa Securities," AMB said in an announcement on Bursa Malaysia today.

items.title: 'No news without journalists': Fahmi rejects proposal for AI-generated news reports
items.summary: SEBERANG JAYA: The government remains committed to ensuring journalists are not marginalised as artificial intelligence (AI) reshapes the media landscape, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today. Speaking at a gathering with Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia (GKMM) and representatives of state media clubs, Fahmi stressed that journalists continue to play an irreplaceable role in society despite rapid technological advances. Fahmi said the government's commitment to the media industry was reflected in the establishment of the Malaysian Media Council, noting that much of the legislation was drafted based on feedback from media practitioners.

items.title: Medical, insurance, takaful practitioners boost healthcare ecosystem via HPPSC platform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Five medical and insurance/takaful associations said initiatives are being implemented to enhance communication and operational efficiency among doctors, hospitals, insurers and takaful operators. The five are the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA), the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia, Life Insurance Association of Malaysia (LIAM), Malaysian Takaful Association, and Persatuan Insurans Am Malaysia. The statememt said a dedicated "doctor-to-doctor" hotline was set up by insurers and takaful operators in April 30, 2026, to support and address claim enquiries and enable direct discussions between medical practitioners and insurers and takaful operators' medical advisory teams.

items.title: Petronas, Turkmenistan strategic partnership opens new chapter in bilateral economic, energy relations
items.summary: ASHGABAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov today witnessed the signing of a strategic partnership between Petronas and Turkmenistan authorities, marking a new chapter in economic and energy relations between the two countries. "The partnership also opens up opportunities for more strategic involvement in developing future energy resources, including exploring the vast potential of Turkmenistan's natural gas sector, which holds some of the world's largest gas reserves," the PMO said. The strategic partnership also reflects international confidence in Malaysia's capabilities in the energy sector and strategic industries.

items.title: JB nasi kandar shop suffers RM200,000 losses in pre-opening paint and arson attack
items.summary: Johor Bahru South district police chief ACP Raub Selamat said police received a report regarding the incident at 3.59 am from a 30-year-old local man who operates the restaurant and based on the restaurant’s closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, the two suspects arrived at about 3.34 am before carrying out the act. Meanwhile, Johor Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 3.43 am and dispatched personnel from the Tebrau Fire and Rescue Station and Kempas Fire and Rescue Station to the scene. However, the fire was successfully extinguished before the arrival of the fire and rescue team using eight fire extinguishers, and no injuries were reported.

items.title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback amid continued expectation for Fed rate hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Friday as persistent expectations of a possible US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike later this year continued to drive the greenback. At 6pm, the local note fell to 4.1340/1395 against the greenback from 4.1145/1195 at Thursday's close. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the prospect of higher US interest rates supported the greenback, exerting pressure on Asian currencies, including the ringgit.

items.title: #NSTviral: Lost wallet row over alleged RM10,000 goes viral
items.summary: The video, uploaded today by TikTok user "C J", showed a couple, believed to be Chinese nationals, demanding that the man return the money allegedly kept inside the wallet. The man repeatedly denied taking any cash inside the wallet. In a subsequent video, the wallet owners said the wallet had been left behind at the supermarket's freezer section after one of them stepped away to answer an urgent phone call.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 19, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, extending gains for a seventh consecutive session, as investors were encouraged by easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Domestically, the market is expected to remain supported by improving economic fundamentals. In the crypto market, Bitcoin fell to RM257,917.

items.title: Delivery rider arrested for allegedly taking RM10,000 from wallet lost at supermarket
items.summary: KAJANG: A 37-year-old delivery rider has been arrested for allegedly stealing RM10,000 in cash from a lost wallet at a supermarket in Semenyih. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received a report on Wednesday night. The arrest follows a viral video showing a woman confronting the delivery rider after he handed back her lost wallet.

items.title: Businesses should identify pain points amid rising uncertainty – Economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian businesses should identify their operational pain points before making strategic decisions as the business landscape becomes increasingly uncertain, said AmBank Group chief economist Firdaos Rosli. He said companies can no longer operate the way they did in the past, as geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions and rapidly evolving risks require businesses to become more resilient and agile. Against this backdrop, Firdaos said Malaysia's economy is expected to remain resilient, although growth would likely moderate over the coming quarters while inflation gradually rises amid higher energy prices and supply chain disruptions.

items.title: MDEC appoints Ganesh Kumar as non-executive chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) has appointed prominent technology entrepreneur and MOL Global Inc founder Ganesh Kumar Bangah as its non-executive chairman. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Ganesh's appointment is expected to further strengthen MDEC's ability to align government priorities with industry execution, given his decades of experience building technology companies, scaling digital platforms and working with entrepreneurs, SMEs and the wider technology ecosystem. "Ganesh's appointment reflects the government's commitment to ensuring that MDEC continues to be guided by strong industry experience and expertise as Malaysia enters our nation's next phase of digital economy growth.

items.title: Malaysia's vehicle sales hit reverse gear in May
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's new vehicle sales, or total industry volume (TIV), fell 15 per cent to 61,250 units in May from 72,113 units in April, ending two straight months of growth. Production fell by 13 per cent year-on-year to 57,489 units in May 2026, compared to 66,354 units in May 2025. Production fell four per cent to 287,477 units from 300,559 units previously.

items.title: PAS takeover complete as ROS confirms new Perikatan leadership with Samsuri as chair
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has officially consolidated its leadership under Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar following formal confirmation from the Registrar of Societies (ROS). First, the department has formally acknowledged the minutes from PN’s extraordinary supreme council meeting held on February 22, 2026. Second, the ROS has officially logged the minutes from the supreme council meeting dated March 14, 2026.

items.title: Lorry lane change triggers East Coast pile-up near Maran involving Zahid’s aides and driver
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 19 — Seven individuals, including a driver and three media officers to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, were involved in a three-vehicle accident at Kilometer 144.2 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) near Maran early this morning. Maran District Police Chief Supt Wong Kim Wai said preliminary investigations revealed that the accident occurred around 1:00 am when a Foton lorry allegedly changed lanes abruptly causing the driver of a Toyota Fortuner heading towards Kuala Lumpur to lose control when trying to avoid but and resulting in a collision with the right side of the lorry. “Another vehicle, a Hicom lorry, which was behind the two vehicles, tried to avoid the accident but lost control before crashing into the road divider,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: CIMB launches regional campaign to drive social progress
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd (CIMB) has unveiled its new regional brand campaign, 'Moving You Forward', reinforcing its commitment to enabling customers, businesses and communities across Asean to achieve meaningful progress. CIMB's chief brand and marketing officer Toni Darusman said the group's ambition extended beyond banking. He said 'Moving You Forward' reflects how CIMB is bringing its purpose of advancing customers and society to life in a more meaningful and impactful way.

items.title: George Kent expands water ops with Techkem acquisition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: George Kent (Malaysia) Bhd is buying a 76 per cent stake in Techkem Group Sdn Bhd for RM2.0 million in cash, strengthening its position in the water and wastewater treatment sector. Techkem specialises in potable water treatment, industrial wastewater treatment, sewerage treatment, wastewater recycling and reclamation, specialty chemicals and sustainable engineering solutions. Over the past decade, Techkem has completed more than 65 water and wastewater treatment installations across the region and developed customised treatment solutions to meet increasingly stringent environmental and sustainability requirements.

items.title: MetMalaysia says rough weather for KL and 10 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit 10 states and Kuala Lumpur until 7 pm today. In Perak, the affected areas are Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim. Similar weather conditions are forecast in Kelantan (covering Kota Bharu, Bachok, Machang and Pasir Puteh), as well as in Terengganu (Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun).

items.title: Vestland unit secures RM65mil works contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Construction company Vestland Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Vestland Infra Sdn Bhd (VISB) has accepted a letter of award from Scanwolf Trading & Construction Sdn Bhd (STCSB) for a works contract worth RM65 million. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, Vestland said the contract involves piling and substructure works for one block of 74-storey service apartment, including car parks, basement, shop lots, and related facilities at Jalan Pavilion here. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RoS confirms PN leadership transition, activities require chairman's consent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Any activity or meeting conducted under PN must obtain the approval of its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar. PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said this follows the Registrar of Societies' (RoS) acknowledgement of its receipt of the minutes of PN's extraordinary Supreme Council meeting held on Feb 22. "RoS also confirmed receipt of the minutes of the Supreme Council Meeting (No.

items.title: Use your own logo and leave: Bersatu info chief says PAS never truly committed to Perikatan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz today said that PAS should leave Perikatan Nasional (PN) and form a new coalition under its own logo and brand, instead of remaining in the opposition pact. In a Facebook post today, Tun Faisal said Bersatu should remain in PN with other component parties and its allies under Ikatan Prihatin Rakyat. Tun Faisal claimed PAS had not been fully committed to PN, citing what he described as political negotiations outside the coalition’s framework.

items.title: What to do when Muslim fathers in Malaysia don’t pay child support? Single mothers’ group proposes blanket sums for quick court relief
items.summary: Since KSIT was founded in 2015, Hajar said the problem of unpaid child support or “nafkah” has become like a “tradition”, as it is a recurring problem every year for the single mothers in the group. At a SIS Forum (Malaysia) forum, KSIT chairman Siti Hajar Mohd Ali said Muslim men should be required to pay monthly child support immediately after divorce. — Picture by Yusof Isa Currently, mothers going through divorce in Malaysia’s Syariah courts have to file separate cases for matters such as divorce, child custody and child maintenance, and it could take time for the court to hear and decide how much the father should pay for the child on a case-by-case basis.

items.title: After five failed attempts, prosecution bid to revive Penang teen Nhaveen’s murder case ends at Federal Court
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 19 — The Federal Court today struck out the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of a man on a charge of murdering bullying victim T. Nhaveen nine years ago. I seek further directions from the court,” said Allan. The appellate court also remitted the case to the Penang High Court for the four men to enter their defence.

items.title: New mosque to rise in front of TRX as Bukit Bintang seeks permanent worship space
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Zulkifli Hassan said the initiative reflects efforts to strengthen Islamic facilities in the Bukit Bintang area, Malay daily Kosmo! He said the project involves a 5.6-acre site located opposite TRX. Zulkifli said the project is expected to become a new landmark in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Teacher among four arrested on suspicion of using Besut house as drug den
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Police have arrested four men, including a teacher, after raiding a house suspected of being used for drug-related activities in Kampung Raja, Besut early yesterday. According to Sinar Harian , Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said the raid was carried out at about 1.30am by the Besut district police headquarters’ Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division following intelligence on suspected drug distribution in the area. Mohd Rozaime said initial urine screening found that three of the four suspects tested positive for Tetrahydrocannabinol, or THC.

items.title: Low water levels at major dams trigger cloud seeding and fire warning in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING, June 19 — The water level at Batang Ai Dam is currently at ‘Event and Drought Emergency Level 1’, while Bakun Dam is at ‘Drought Emergency Level 1’, revealed Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He said the Sarawak government has launched another round of cloud seeding operations in collaboration with the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) focussing on major dams and water catchment areas across the state. Uggah, who is also State Disaster Management Committee (SDMC) chairman, said a similar operation conducted in May had successfully increased water levels at Batang Ai Dam, although water levels at Bakun Dam remained a concern.

items.title: High Court sets aside subpoena for Anwar to testify in RM30m civil suit against businessman and wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The High Court here has set aside a subpoena requiring Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to appear as a witness in a RM30 million civil suit filed by 12 individuals against businessman Datuk Vinod Balachandra Sekhar and his wife, Winny Yeap Liew Heoh. Judicial Commissioner Datuk Muhammad Adam @ Edward Abdullah, in allowing Anwar’s application to set aside the subpoena issued on January 15, ruled that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the Prime Minister was a relevant and material witness to the proceedings. “The plaintiffs relied on photographs, social media postings and public appearances involving Anwar and Vinod.

items.title: Chief Justice: Public trust is judiciary’s most valuable asset, must be earned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Public trust remains the judiciary’s most valuable asset and must be earned through integrity, accountability and consistent conduct, said Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh. Drawing on the legacy of former Chief Justice Tun Zaki Azmi, he said institutional leaders must be prepared to make difficult decisions and work to leave behind stronger, more trusted institutions for future generations. Elaborating on the objectives of the lecture series, he said the Tun Zaki Lectures on Leadership and Stewardship and the Leadership and Stewardship Research Initiative were intended to promote meaningful dialogue on leadership and develop practical scholarship relevant to the realities of institutional leadership.

items.title: Court sentences grandfather to 33 years jail, 14 strokes for raping and sexually assaulting his granddaughters
items.summary: The man, who has three children, was accused in the first and fourth charges of committing sexual assault against his 12-year-old granddaughter at a house in Batu Pahat. The offences were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16 of the same Act. The fifth charge involved the sexual assault of another granddaughter, aged nine, at the same location last year.

items.title: Immigration DG: Actual number of refugees in Malaysia higher than UNHCR’s 200,000 figure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The Immigration Department today said the actual number of refugees in Malaysia is believed to be higher than the roughly 200,000 individuals currently recorded by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR). According to Kosmo , Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the government is carrying out a comprehensive registration exercise involving all refugees in the country, including those without UNHCR cards. Zakaria said this at a press conference after the passing-out parade for the 2026 Series 1 Basic Immigration and Higher Immigration Certificate courses at the Malaysian Immigration Academy in Port Dickson today.

items.title: Selangor hawkers to get six-month rent discount from July under state relief package
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 19 — The Selangor government will provide a 30 per cent rental reduction for hawkers and traders operating at Local Authority (PBT) premises under Phase Two of the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package. “This assistance will benefit 21,234 traders with an allocation of RM4.5 million from the state government. Amirudin said that under the same package, the state government will also introduce a three-month moratorium for 1,012 tenants of the Selangor Smart Sewa scheme beginning in October.

items.title: Pakatan top leaders will decide Puteri Wangsa seat dispute, says PKR sec-gen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) leadership will hold talks soon to resolve a dispute over the Puteri Wangsa state seat ahead of the Johor state election. Yesterday, Amanah strongly objected to the Puteri Wangsa seat being handed over to PKR in the upcoming state election. He said Johor Amanah has made early preparations and has its machinery ready to reclaim and defend the Puteri Wangsa state seat.

items.title: As pigeon-munching croc settles near Kuching floating mosque, Sarawak minister urges forestry agency to act
items.summary: KUCHING, June 19 — Dato Sri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah has called on the Sarawak Forestry Corporation (SFC) to take immediate action following the sighting of a large crocodile in the Sarawak River near the Masjid India floating mosque. The Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts acknowledged the complex situation, noting the crocodile’s presence carries a dual nature. “Well, we do know of the existence of the crocodiles in the Sarawak River, especially under the floating mosque.

items.title: PKR puts Rafizi, Nik Nazmi exit under legal review, Wong Chen faces disciplinary process
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR will refer any legal considerations involving former Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad to its appointed legal team, while also passing a separate disciplinary matter involving Subang MP Wong Chen to the party’s disciplinary board. PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said any follow-up action involving Rafizi and Nik Nazmi, who have both left the party, will be handled by party lawyers. “Both have already resigned and left the party.

items.title: Selangor expands RM100m financing scheme to help contractors weather supply crunch
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 19 — The Selangor state government plans to expand its Selangor Advance (SA500) programme to support the cash flow needs of companies involved in state infrastructure projects, including those undertaken at the district and local authority levels. “With an allocation of RM100 million, the initiative is expected to benefit 500 companies and contractors operating in Selangor,” he said while tabling the package at a special sitting of the Selangor State Legislative Assembly here today. Amirudin said the state government will also introduce interest-free financing of up to RM10,000 for micro, small and medium entrepreneurs through a collaboration between Yayasan Hijrah Selangor (Hijrah) and Platform Selangor (PLATS).

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim, in role as police honorary commissioner, receives security briefing from top cop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail at Istana Bukit Tunku here. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, the audience was held in His Majesty’s capacity as the Honorary Commissioner of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). During the audience, Sultan Ibrahim was briefed by Mohd Khalid on a range of matters relating to national security and public order.

items.title: PKR sec-gen confirms party candidates’ list ‘99pc’ ready for Johor, Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR’s list of candidates for the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections is 99 per cent complete, party secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said today. She confirmed that PKR will contest 20 state seats in Johor and 16 in Negeri Sembilan under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner. The Negeri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved on June 5.

items.title: Sabah first: Ewon says Upko's GRS entry is about standing as strong as Sarawak
items.summary: KOTA BELUD, June 19 — United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) President Datuk Ewon Benedick said the party must continue to play a role in strengthening unity and cooperation among Sabah-based political parties, in line with the spirit of “Sabah First”, which remains one of its core struggles. “Let us take the lead, and Kadamaian should contribute towards fostering unity and cooperation among Sabah’s local parties. This is what we mean by Sabah First,” he said.

items.title: Transport minister says JPJ has bigger road safety concerns than car sunshades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today clarified that the Road Transport Department (JPJ) is not prioritising enforcement against vehicle sunshades, following public confusion over whether removable sunshades and curtains are allowed on car windows. According to Berita Harian , Loke said while existing rules allow action to be taken if such accessories breach road transport regulations, JPJ’s focus remains on offences that pose a more serious threat to road users. “There are many road offences, but there are more critical safety-related matters that are JPJ’s priority.

items.title: Hadi keeps mum on Perikatan turmoil, says Ahmad Samsuri’s statement is ‘enough’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has declined to comment on current developments in Perikatan Nasional (PN), including the position of PAS and Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) in the coalition. According to Utusan Malaysia , the Marang MP said PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar’s recent statement on the coalition was sufficient for now. “Samsuri’s statement is enough.

items.title: Police probe suspected robbery after teen girl found injured in Tumpat
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 19 — A 15-year-old girl sustained serious injuries in a suspected robbery at Kampung Mesira, Palekbang, Tumpat, on Wednesday. “The victim was taken to Tumpat Hospital for initial treatment before being transferred to Raja Permaisuri Zainab II Hospital in Kota Bharu for further treatment by specialists,” he said in a statement yesterday. Mohd Khairi said initial investigations revealed that the victim was allegedly hit on the face and head with a stick by a suspect.

items.title: Reported missing from Johor at 15, woman now 21 found with child in Langkawi
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 19 — A woman who had been reported missing since 2019 after leaving her family home in Johor Bahru was found safe at a house in Taman Helang Perdana, Kuah, Wednesday. “Further checks based on missing persons records found that the woman was still listed as a missing person following a police report lodged in Majidee, Johor, in 2019. “The woman admitted to leaving her family home in Johor Bahru before settling in Langkawi.

items.title: Mara to place former soldiers as full-time wardens at all MRSM institutions from next January
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will place full-time wardens from among former soldiers at all 58 Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) nationwide from January 1, 2027. Mara chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the initiative would begin with 10 MRSM from July 1, after the selection and screening of candidates are carried out throughout this month. “A study by Universiti Putra Malaysia showed that the pilot project placing full-time wardens from among former soldiers at MRSM Besut and MRSM Balik Pulau had successfully improved student discipline and even achieved zero bullying,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Anwar praises Putin, Xi for condemning Gaza, Iran attacks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has commended Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping for advocating peace and condemning atrocities against the people of Gaza, Iran and Lebanon. Anwar said Malaysia has consistently taken a firm position against attacks by the Zionist regime while upholding its principles independently of any superpower. “We took a decision in Parliament, bipartisan, and continued to engage,” he said during an exclusive interview with Russian international television network RT.

items.title: Anwar pushes trade, AI and energy ties between Asean and Russia
items.summary: KAZAN, June 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Asean and Russia to strengthen cooperation in various strategic areas, while stressing that dialogue and diplomacy must continue to be the foundation in handling conflicts and geopolitical tensions. Anwar also stressed the importance of forging closer cooperation in trade and investment, digital, science and technology, artificial intelligence (AI), energy, food security, the halal industry, and people-to-people exchanges to generate greater benefits for Asean countries and Russia. The two-day summit represents the highest platform for Asean and Russia to discuss strategic cooperation and exchange views on regional and international issues of mutual importance.
